Identification.LongTitle: An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and to make amendments to other Acts
Identification.ShortTitle: An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and to make amendments to other Acts
Identification.RunningHead: An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection ...
Identification.BillSponsor: MINISTER OF ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AND DIGITAL INNOVATION

Provision.Text: This enactment enacts the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act to govern the protection of personal information of individuals while taking into account the need of organizations to collect, use or disclose personal information in the course of commercial activities. Consequently, it repeals Part 1 of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and changes the short title of that Act to the Electronic Documents Act. Finally, it also makes amendments to other Acts.

Heading.TitleText: An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and to make amendments to other Acts

Heading.Label: PART 1
Heading.TitleText: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act
An Act to support and promote digital commerce by protecting personal information that is collected, used or disclosed in the course of commercial activities

Replacement of âPersonal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Actâ

Provision.Text: His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

TitleText.XRefExternal: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act

Section.Text: The Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, whose text is as follows and whose schedule is set out in the schedule to this Act, is enacted:

BillInternal.LongTitle: An Act to support and promote digital commerce by protecting personal information that is collected, used or disclosed in the course of commercial activities

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this Act.

Definition.Text: alternative format, with respect to personal information, means a format that allows an individual with a sensory disability to read or listen to the personal information.â(support de substitution)

anonymizeâmeans to irreversibly and permanently modify personal information to ensure that there is no reasonably foreseeable risk in the circumstances that an individual can be identified from the information, whether directly or indirectly, by any means.â(anonymiser)

automated decision systemâmeans any technology that assists or replaces the judgment of human decision-makers through the use of a rules-based system, regression analysis, predictive analytics, machine learning, deep learning, a neural network or other technique.â(systÃ¨me dÃ©cisionnel automatisÃ©)

breach of security safeguardsâmeans the loss of, unauthorized access to or unauthorized disclosure of personal information resulting from a breach of an organizationâs security safeguards that are referred to in section 56 or from a failure to establish those safeguards.â(atteinte aux mesures de sÃ©curitÃ©)
business transactionâincludes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purchase, sale or other acquisition or disposition of an organization or a part of an organization, or any of its assets;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the merger or amalgamation of two or more organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the making of a loan or provision of other financing to an organization or a part of an organization;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the creating of a charge on, or the taking of a security interest in or a security on, any assets or securities of an organization;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the lease or licensing of any of an organizationâs assets; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other prescribed arrangement between two or more organizations to conduct a business activity.â(transaction commerciale)

Definition.Text: Chairpersonâmeans the Chairperson of the Commission, as designated under subsection 17(1) of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act.â(prÃ©sident)
childâmeans an individual who is under 18 years of age.â(enfant)

commercial activityâmeans any particular transaction, act or conduct or any regular course of conduct that is of a commercial character, including the selling, bartering or leasing of donor, membership or other fundraising lists.â(activitÃ© commerciale)
Commissionâmeans the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada referred to in section 4 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act.â(Commission)
Commissionerâmeans the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner, as designated under subsection 85(1).â(commissaire)
de-identifyâmeans to modify personal information so that an individual cannot be directly identified from it, although a risk of the individual being identified remains.â(dÃ©personnaliser)
Divisionâmeans the Privacy and Consumer Data Division established under section 89.â(Section)
disposeâmeans to permanently and irreversibly delete personal information or to anonymize it.â(retrait)
federal work, undertaking or businessâmeans any work, undertaking or business that is within the legislative authority of Parliament. It includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a work, undertaking or business that is operated or carried on for or in connection with navigation and shipping, whether inland or maritime, including the operation of ships and transportation by ship anywhere in Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a railway, canal, telegraph or other work or undertaking that connects a province with another province, or that extends beyond the limits of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a line of ships that connects a province with another province, or that extends beyond the limits of a province;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a ferry between a province and another province or between a province and a country other than Canada;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: aerodromes, aircraft or a line of air transportation;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: a radio broadcasting station;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: a bank or an authorized foreign bank as those terms are defined in section 2 of the Bank Act;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: a work that, although wholly situated within a province, is before or after its execution declared by Parliament to be for the general advantage of Canada or for the advantage of two or more provinces;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: a work, undertaking or business outside the exclusive legislative authority of the legislatures of the provinces; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: a work, undertaking or business to which federal laws, as defined in section 2 of the Oceans Act, apply under section 20 of that Act and any regulations made under paragraph 26(1)(k) of that Act.â(entreprises fÃ©dÃ©rales)

Definition.Text: Ministerâmeans the member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada designated under section 3 or, if no member is designated, the Minister of Industry.â(ministre)
organizationâincludes an association, a partnership, a person or a trade union.â(organisation)
personal informationâmeans information about an identifiable individual, including information that is inferred about the individual.â(renseignement personnel)
prescribedâmeans prescribed by regulation.â(Version anglaise seulement)
recordâmeans any documentary material, regardless of medium or form.â(document)

sensitiveâdescribes personal information in respect of which, taking into account the circumstances, an individual has a heightened expectation of privacy, including, as the case may be, a childâs personal information, personal information revealing an individualâs racial or ethnic origin, political opinions or religious or philosophical beliefs, an individualâs trade union membership, genetic information or health information, biometric information that is capable of uniquely identifying the individual or information concerning an individualâs sexual orientation.â(caractÃ¨re sensible)

service providerâmeans an organization, including a parent corporation, subsidiary, affiliate, contractor or subcontractor, that provides services for or on behalf of another organization to assist the organization in fulfilling its purposes.â(fournisseur de services)

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, de-identified personal information does not cease to be personal information.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate any member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada to be the Minister for the purposes of this Act.
The rights provided for under this Act, including any right of recourse, may be exercised

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: on behalf of a child, by a parent, guardian or tutor, unless the child wishes to personally exercise those rights and is capable of doing so;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: on behalf of an individual, other than a child, under a legal incapacity, by a person authorized by law to administer the affairs or property of that individual;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: on behalf of a deceased individual, by a person authorized by law to administer the estate or succession of that individual, but only for the purpose of that administration; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: on behalf of any other individual, by a person authorized in writing by that individual to do so.

Section.Text: The purpose of this Act is to establish â in an era in which data is constantly flowing across borders and geographical boundaries and significant economic activity relies on the analysis, circulation and exchange of personal information â rules to govern the protection of personal information in a manner that recognizes the fundamental right of privacy of individuals with respect to their personal information and the need of organizations to collect, use or disclose personal information for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate in the circumstances.

Subsection.Text: This Act applies to every organization in respect of personal information that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the organization collects, uses or discloses in the course of commercial activities; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is about an employee of, or an applicant for employment with, the organization and that the organization collects, uses or discloses in connection with the operation of a federal work, undertaking or business.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, this Act applies in respect of personal information

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that is collected, used or disclosed interprovincially or internationally by an organization; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that is collected, used or disclosed by an organization within a province, to the extent that the organization is not exempt from the application of this Act under an order made under paragraph 139(2)(b).

Subsection.Text: This Act also applies to an organization set out in column 1 of the schedule in respect of personal information set out in column 2.
This Act does not apply to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: any government institution to which the Privacy Act applies;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any individual in respect of personal information that the individual collects, uses or discloses solely for personal or domestic purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any organization in respect of personal information that the organization collects, uses or discloses solely for journalistic, artistic or literary purposes;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any organization in respect of an individualâs personal information that the organization collects, uses or discloses solely for the purpose of communicating or facilitating communication with the individual in relation to their employment, business or profession; or

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: any organization that is, under an order made under paragraph 139(2)(b), exempt from the application of this Act in respect of the collection, use or disclosure of personal information that occurs within a province in respect of which the order was made.

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, this Act does not apply in respect of anonymized information.

Every provision of this Act applies despite any provision, enacted after December 31, 2000, of any other Act of Parliament, unless the other Act expressly declares that that provision operates despite the provision of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Accountability â personal information under organizationâs control

Subsection.Text: An organization is accountable for personal information that is under its control.

Personal information is under the control of the organization that decides to collect it and that determines the purposes for its collection, use or disclosure, regardless of whether the information is collected, used or disclosed by the organization itself or by a service provider on behalf of the organization.

An organization must designate one or more individuals to be responsible for matters related to its obligations under this Act. It must provide the designated individualâs business contact information to any person who requests it.
The designation of an individual under subsection (1) does not relieve the organization of its obligations under this Act.

Every organization must implement and maintain a privacy management program that includes the policies, practices and procedures the organization has put in place to fulfill its obligations under this Act, including policies, practices and procedures respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the protection of personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: how requests for information and complaints are received and dealt with;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the training and information provided to the organizationâs staff respecting its policies, practices and procedures; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the development of materials to explain the organizationâs policies and procedures.

Subsection.Text: In developing its privacy management program, the organization must take into account the volume of personal information under its control and the sensitivity of that information.
An organization must, on request, provide the Commission with access to the policies, practices and procedures that are included in its privacy management program.

The Commission may, after reviewing the policies, practices and procedures, provide guidance on, or recommend that corrective measures be taken by the organization in relation to, its privacy management program.

If an organization transfers personal information to a service provider, the organization must ensure, by contract or otherwise, that the service provider provides a level of protection in respect of the personal information equivalent to that which the organization is required to provide under this Act.

The obligations under this Part, other than those set out in sections 56 and 61, do not apply to a service provider in respect of personal information that is transferred to it. However, the service provider is subject to all of the obligations under this Part if it collects, uses or discloses that information for any purpose other than the purposes for which the information was transferred to it.

An organization may collect, use or disclose personal information only in a manner and for purposes that a reasonable person would consider appropriate in the circumstances, whether or not consent is required under this Act.
All relevant factors must be taken into account in determining whether the manner and purposes referred to in subsection (1) are appropriate, including, if applicable

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the sensitivity of the personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the purposes represent legitimate business needs of the organization;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the degree of effectiveness of the collection, use or disclosure in meeting the organizationâs legitimate business needs;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether there are less intrusive means of achieving those purposes at a comparable cost and with comparable benefits; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: whether the individualâs loss of privacy is proportionate to the benefits in light of the measures, technical or otherwise, implemented by the organization to mitigate the impacts of the loss of privacy on the individual.

Subsection.Text: The organization must determine at or before the time of the collection of any personal information each of the purposes for which the information is or is to be collected, used or disclosed and record those purposes.

If the organization determines that the personal information it has collected is to be used or disclosed for a new purpose, the organization must record that new purpose before using or disclosing that information for the new purpose.

Section.Text: An organization may collect only the personal information that is necessary for the purposes determined and recorded under subsection 12(3).

Subsection.Text: An organization must not use or disclose personal information for a purpose other than a purpose determined and recorded under subsection 12(3), unless the organization obtains the individualâs valid consent before any use or disclosure for that other purpose.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Use or disclosure â other purposes
Subsection.Text: Despite subsection (1), an organization may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: use personal information for a purpose other than a purpose determined and recorded under subsection 12(3) in any of the circumstances set out in sections 18, 20 and 21, subsections 22(1) and (3) and sections 23, 24, 26, 30, 39 and 50; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: disclose personal information for a purpose other than a purpose determined and recorded under subsection 12(3) in any of the circumstances set out in section 18, subsections 22(1) and (3), sections 23 to 28 and 31 to 36, subsection 38(3) and sections 40 to 50.

Subsection.Text: Unless this Act provides otherwise, an organization must obtain an individualâs valid consent for the collection, use or disclosure of the individualâs personal information.

The individualâs consent must be obtained at or before the time of the collection of the personal information or, if the information is to be used or disclosed for a purpose other than a purpose determined and recorded under subsection 12(3), before any use or disclosure of the information for that other purpose.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information for consent to be valid
Subsection.Text: The individualâs consent is valid only if, at or before the time that the organization seeks the individualâs consent, the organization provides the individual with the following information:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purposes for the collection, use or disclosure of the personal information determined by the organization and recorded under subsection 12(3) or (4);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the manner in which the personal information is to be collected, used or disclosed;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any reasonably foreseeable consequences of the collection, use or disclosure of the personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the specific type of personal information that is to be collected, used or disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the names of any third parties or types of third parties to which the organization may disclose the personal information.

Subsection.Text: The organization must provide the information referred to in subsection (3) in plain language that an individual to whom the organizationâs activities are directed would reasonably be expected to understand.

Consent must be expressly obtained unless, subject to subsection (6), it is appropriate to rely on an individualâs implied consent, taking into account the reasonable expectations of the individual and the sensitivity of the personal information that is to be collected, used or disclosed.

It is not appropriate to rely on an individualâs implied consent if their personal information is collected or used for an activity described in subsection 18(2) or if it is collected, used or disclosed for an activity described in subsection 18(3).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Consent â provision of product or service
Subsection.Text: The organization must not, as a condition of the provision of a product or service, require an individual to consent to the collection, use or disclosure of their personal information beyond what is necessary to provide the product or service.

Section.Text: An organization must not obtain or attempt to obtain an individualâs consent by providing false or misleading information or using deceptive or misleading practices. Any consent obtained under those circumstances is invalid.

Subsection.Text: On giving reasonable notice to an organization, an individual may, at any time, subject to this Act, to federal or provincial law or to the reasonable terms of a contract, withdraw their consent in whole or in part.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Collection, use or disclosure to cease
Subsection.Text: On receiving the notice from the individual, the organization must inform the individual of the consequences of the withdrawal of their consent and, as soon as feasible after that, cease the collection, use or disclosure of the individualâs personal information in respect of which the consent was withdrawn.

An organization may collect or use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the collection or use is made for the purpose of a business activity described in subsection (2) and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reasonable person would expect the collection or use for such an activity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the personal information is not collected or used for the purpose of influencing the individualâs behaviour or decisions.

Subsection.Text: Subject to the regulations, the following activities are business activities for the purposes of subsection (1):

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an activity that is necessary to provide a product or service that the individual has requested from the organization;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an activity that is necessary for the security of the organizationâs information, systems or networks;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: an activity that is necessary for the safety of a product or service that the organization provides; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: any other prescribed activity.

Subsection.Text: An organization may collect, use or disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the collection, use or disclosure is made for the purpose of an activity in which the organization has a legitimate interest that outweighs any reasonably foreseeable adverse effect on the individual that could result from that collection, use or disclosure and

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a reasonable person would expect the collection, use or disclosure for such an activity; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the personal information is not collected, used or disclosed for the purpose of influencing the individualâs behaviour or decisions.

Subsection.Text: Before collecting, using or disclosing personal information under subsection (3), the organization must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: identify and describe its legitimate interest in the proposed activity;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: carry out, in accordance with the prescribed requirements, a privacy impact assessment in which the organization must identify any reasonably foreseeable adverse effect on the individual that is likely to result from the collection, use or disclosure;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: identify and take reasonable measures to reduce the likelihood that the effects will occur or to mitigate or eliminate them; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: comply with any prescribed requirements.

Subsection.Text: The organization must record the description of its legitimate interest and must, on request, provide the Commission with access to, or a copy of, the description and assessment.

Section.Text: An organization may transfer an individualâs personal information to a service provider without their knowledge or consent.
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to de-identify or anonymize the information.

An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the organizationâs internal research, analysis and development purposes if the information is de-identified before it is used.

Subsection.Text: Organizations that are parties to a prospective business transaction may use and disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information is de-identified before it is used or disclosed and remains so until the transaction is completed;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the organizations have entered into an agreement that requires the organization that receives the information

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to use and disclose that information solely for purposes related to the transaction,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to protect the information by security safeguards proportionate to the sensitivity of the information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the transaction does not proceed, to return the information to the organization that disclosed it, or dispose of it, within a reasonable time;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the organizations comply with the terms of that agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the information is necessary

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to determine whether to proceed with the transaction, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the determination is made to proceed with the transaction, to complete it.

Subsection.Text: The requirement referred to in paragraph (1)(a) does not apply if it would undermine the objectives for carrying out the transaction and the organization has taken into account the risk of harm to the individual that could result from using or disclosing the information.

If the business transaction is completed, the organizations that are parties to the transaction may use and disclose the personal information referred to in subsection (1) without the individualâs knowledge or consent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the organizations have entered into an agreement that requires each of them

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: to use and disclose the information under its control solely for the purposes for which the information was collected or permitted to be used or disclosed before the transaction was completed,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: to protect that information by security safeguards proportionate to the sensitivity of the information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: to give effect to any withdrawal of consent made under subsection 17(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the organizations comply with the terms of that agreement;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information is necessary for carrying on the business or activity that was the object of the transaction; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: one of the parties notifies the individual, within a reasonable time after the transaction is completed, that the transaction has been completed and that their information has been disclosed under subsection (1).

Subsection.Text: Subsections (1) and (3) do not apply to a business transaction of which the primary purpose or result is the purchase, sale or other acquisition or disposition, or lease, of personal information.

Section.MarginalNote: Information produced in employment, business or profession
Section.Text: An organization may collect, use or disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it was produced by the individual in the course of their employment, business or profession and the collection, use or disclosure is consistent with the purposes for which the information was produced.

Section.MarginalNote: Employment relationship â federal work, undertaking or business
Section.Text: An organization that operates a federal work, undertaking or business may collect, use or disclose an individualâs personal information without their consent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the collection, use or disclosure is necessary to establish, manage or terminate an employment relationship between the organization and the individual in connection with the operation of a federal work, undertaking or business; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the organization has informed the individual that the personal information will be or may be collected, used or disclosed for those purposes.

Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a lawyer or, in Quebec, a lawyer or notary, who is representing the organization.

An organization may collect, use or disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the information is contained in a witness statement and the collection, use or disclosure is necessary to assess, process or settle an insurance claim.

Section.MarginalNote: Prevention, detection or suppression of fraud

Subsection.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information to another organization without the individualâs knowledge or consent if the disclosure is reasonable for the purpose of detecting or suppressing fraud or of preventing fraud that is likely to be committed and it is reasonable to expect that the disclosure with the individualâs knowledge or consent would compromise the ability to prevent, detect or suppress the fraud.
An organization may collect or use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the information was disclosed to it under subsection (1).

Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the purpose of collecting a debt owed by the individual to the organization.

Subsection.Text: An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the collection is clearly in the interests of the individual and consent cannot be obtained in a timely way.
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the information was collected under subsection (1).

Section.Text: An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the purpose of acting in respect of an emergency that threatens the life, health or security of any individual.

An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a person who needs the information because of an emergency that threatens the life, health or security of any individual. If the individual whom the information is about is alive, the organization must, in writing and without delay, inform that individual of the disclosure.

An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the disclosure is necessary to identify the individual who is injured, ill or deceased and is made to a government institution, a part of a government institution or the individualâs next of kin or authorized representative. If the individual is alive, the organization must, in writing and without delay, inform them of the disclosure.

Section.MarginalNote: Communication with next of kin or authorized representative
Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or part of a government institution that has made a request for the information, identified its lawful authority to obtain the information and indicated that the disclosure is requested for the purpose of communicating with the next of kin or authorized representative of an injured, ill or deceased individual.

An organization may on its own initiative disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution, a part of a government institution or the individualâs next of kin or authorized representative if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the organization has reasonable grounds to believe that the individual has been, is or may be the victim of financial abuse;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is made solely for purposes related to preventing or investigating the abuse; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: it is reasonable to expect that disclosure with the knowledge or consent of the individual would compromise the ability to prevent or investigate the abuse.

Section.MarginalNote: Records of historical or archival importance
Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to an institution whose functions include the conservation of records of historical or archival importance, if the disclosure is made for the purpose of such conservation.
An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent after the earlier of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: 100 years after the record containing the information was created, and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: 20 years after the death of the individual.

Section.Text: An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the collection is solely for journalistic, artistic or literary purposes.

Subsection.Text: An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it is reasonable to expect that the collection with their knowledge or consent would compromise the availability or the accuracy of the information and the collection is reasonably made for a purpose related to investigating a breach of an agreement or a contravention of federal or provincial law.
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the information was collected under subsection (1).

An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the disclosure is reasonably made to another organization for a purpose related to investigating a breach of an agreement or a contravention of federal or provincial law that has been, is being or is about to be committed and it is reasonable to expect that disclosure with the individualâs knowledge or consent would compromise the investigation.

Section.Text: An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if, in the course of its activities, the organization becomes aware of information that it has reasonable grounds to believe could be useful in the investigation of a contravention of federal or provincial law or a law of a foreign jurisdiction that has been, is being or is about to be committed and the information is used for the purpose of investigating that contravention.
An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is made to the other organization, government institution or part of a government institution that was notified of a breach under subsection 59(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the disclosure is made solely for the purpose of reducing the risk of harm to the individual that could result from the breach or mitigating that harm.

MarginalNote.XRefExternal: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Subsection.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to another organization under subsection 11.01(1) of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act.
An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the personal information is disclosed to it under subsection (1).
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it was collected under subsection (2).

Section.MarginalNote: Administering law â request of government institution
Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or part of a government institution that has made a request for the information, identified its lawful authority to obtain the information and indicated that the disclosure is requested for the purpose of administering federal or provincial law.

Section.MarginalNote: Law enforcement â request of government institution
Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or part of a government institution that has made a request for the information, identified its lawful authority to obtain the information and indicated that the disclosure is requested for the purpose of enforcing federal or provincial law or the law of a foreign jurisdiction, carrying out an investigation relating to the enforcement of any such law or gathering intelligence for the purpose of enforcing any such law.

Section.MarginalNote: Contravention of law â initiative of organization
Section.Text: An organization may on its own initiative disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or a part of a government institution if the organization has reasonable grounds to believe that the information relates to a contravention of federal or provincial law or the law of a foreign jurisdiction that has been, is being or is about to be committed.

MarginalNote.XRefExternal: Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act

Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to the government institution referred to in section 7 of the Proceeds of Crime (Money Laundering) and Terrorist Financing Act as required by that section.

Section.MarginalNote: National security, defence or international affairs â request by government institution

Subsection.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or part of a government institution that has made a request for the information, identified its lawful authority to obtain the information and indicated that it suspects that the information relates to national security, the defence of Canada or the conduct of international affairs.
An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the purpose of making a disclosure under subsection (1).
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it was collected under subsection (2).

Section.MarginalNote: National security, defence or international affairs â initiative of organization

Subsection.Text: An organization may on its own initiative disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent to a government institution or a part of a government institution if the organization suspects that the information relates to national security, the defence of Canada or the conduct of international affairs.
An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the purpose of making a disclosure under subsection (1).
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it was collected under subsection (2).
An organization may collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent for the purpose of making a disclosure that is required by law.
An organization may use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if it was collected under subsection (1).
An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the disclosure is required by law.

Section.Text: An organization may disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the disclosure is required to comply with a subpoena or warrant issued or an order made by a court, person or body with jurisdiction to compel the production of information, or to comply with rules of procedure relating to the production of records.

An organization may collect, use or disclose an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent if the personal information is publicly available and is specified by the regulations.

Heading.TitleText: Non-application of Certain Exceptions â Electronic Addresses and Computer Systems

Subsection.Text: The following definitions apply in this section.

Definition.Text: accessâmeans to program, execute programs on, communicate with, store data in, retrieve data from or otherwise make use of any resources, including data or programs of a computer system or a computer network.â(utiliser)
computer programâhas the same meaning as in subsection 342.1(2) of the Criminal Code.â(programme dâordinateur)
computer systemâhas the same meaning as in subsection 342.1(2) of the Criminal Code.â(ordinateur)
electronic addressâmeans an address used in connection with

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: an electronic mail account;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an instant messaging account; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any similar account.â(adresse Ã©lectronique)

Subsection.MarginalNote: Collection and use of electronic addresses
Subsection.Text: An organization is not authorized under section 18, 23 or 26, subsection 29(1) or section 30, 37, 39 or 50 to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collect an individualâs electronic address without their knowledge or consent, if the address is collected by the use of a computer program that is designed or marketed primarily for use in generating or searching for, and collecting, electronic addresses; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use an individualâs electronic address without their knowledge or consent, if the address is collected by the use of a computer program described in paragraph (a).

Subsection.MarginalNote: Collection and use of personal information
Subsection.Text: An organization is not authorized under section 18, 23 or 26, subsection 29(1), section 30 or 37, subsection 38(1) or section 39 or 50 to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: collect an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent, through any means of telecommunication, if the information is collected by accessing a computer system or causing a computer system to be accessed in contravention of an Act of Parliament; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: use an individualâs personal information without their knowledge or consent, if the information is collected in a manner described in paragraph (a).

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 15(5), an organization is not to rely on an individualâs implied consent in respect of any collection of personal information described in paragraph (2)(a) or (3)(a) or any use of personal information described in paragraph (2)(b) or (3)(b).

Heading.TitleText: Retention and Disposal of Personal Information

Subsection.Text: An organization must not retain personal information for a period longer than necessary to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: fulfill the purposes for which the information was collected, used or disclosed; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: comply with the requirements of this Act, of federal or provincial law or of the reasonable terms of a contract.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The organization must dispose of the information as soon as feasible after that period.

Subsection.Text: For the purposes of paragraph (1)(a), when determining the retention period, the organization must take into account the sensitivity of the information.

Section.Text: An organization that uses personal information to make a decision about an individual must retain the information for a sufficient period of time to permit the individual to make a request for access under section 63.

Subsection.Text: If an organization receives a written request from an individual to dispose of their personal information that is under the organizationâs control, the organization must, as soon as feasible, dispose of the information, if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information was collected, used or disclosed in contravention of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has withdrawn their consent, in whole or in part, to the collection, use or disclosure of the information; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information is no longer necessary for the continued provision of a product or service requested by the individual.

Subsection.Text: An organization may refuse a request to dispose of personal information in the circumstances described in paragraph (1)(b) or (c) if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the disposal of the information would result in the disposal of personal information about another individual and the information cannot be severed without imposing an undue burden on the organization;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: there are other requirements of this Act, of federal or provincial law or of the reasonable terms of a contract that prevent it from disposing of the information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the information is necessary for the establishment of a legal defence or in the exercise of other legal remedies by the organization;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the information is not in relation to a child and the disposal of the information would have an undue adverse effect on the accuracy or integrity of information that is necessary to the ongoing provision of a product or service to the individual in question;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the request is vexatious or made in bad faith; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the disposal of the information would have an undue adverse effect on the organization that outweighs any potential adverse effect on the individual resulting from the retention of the information.

Subsection.Text: An organization is not required to dispose of de-identified personal information.
An organization that refuses to dispose of an individualâs personal information must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: inform them in writing of the refusal, setting out the reasons and any recourse that they may have under section 73 or subsection 97(1); and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of a request that is refused under paragraph (2)(f), inform the Commission in writing of the refusal, setting out the reasons.

Subsection.Text: If an organization disposes of personal information at an individualâs request, it must, as soon as feasible, inform any service provider to which it has transferred the information of the request and ensure that the service provider disposes of the information.

An organization must take reasonable steps to ensure that personal information under its control is as accurate, up to date and complete as is necessary to fulfill the purposes for which the information is collected, used or disclosed.
An organization is not required to take the steps referred to in subsection (1) in respect of de-identified personal information.
In determining the extent to which personal information must be accurate, complete and up to date, the organization must take into account the individualâs interests, including

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: whether the information may be used to make a decision about the individual;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: whether the information is used on an ongoing basis; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether the information is disclosed to third parties.

Subsection.Text: An organization is not to routinely update personal information unless it is necessary to fulfill the purposes for which the information is collected, used or disclosed.

An organization must protect personal information through physical, organizational and technological security safeguards. The level of protection provided by those safeguards must be proportionate to the sensitivity of the information.

In addition to the sensitivity of the information, the organization must, in establishing its security safeguards, take into account the quantity, distribution, format and method of storage of the information, as well as any reasonably foreseeable privacy implications that may arise in relation to the transfer of personal information to a service provider.

The security safeguards must protect personal information against, among other things, loss, theft or unauthorized access, disclosure, copying, use or modification and must include reasonable measures to authenticate the identity of the individual to whom the personal information relates.

Section.MarginalNote: Disclosure or transfer outside of Canada

Subsection.Text: Before disclosing or transferring personal information outside of Canada, an organization must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: carry out, in accordance with the prescribed requirements, a privacy impact assessment; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: implement measures to mitigate the risks identified in the privacy impact assessment, such as contractual privacy protection measures, adherence to a code of practice or certification process approved by the Division or any other prescribed measures.

Subsection.Text: An organization must, on request, provide the Commission with access to, or a copy of, the assessment.

An organization must report to the Commission any breach of security safeguards involving personal information under the organizationâs control if it is reasonable in the circumstances to believe that the breach creates a real risk of significant harm to an individual.
The report must contain the prescribed information and must be made in the prescribed form and manner as soon as feasible after the organization determines that the breach has occurred.

Unless otherwise prohibited by law, an organization must notify an individual of any breach of security safeguards involving the individualâs personal information under the organizationâs control if it is reasonable in the circumstances to believe that the breach creates a real risk of significant harm to the individual.

The notification must contain sufficient information to allow the individual to understand the significance to them of the breach and to take steps, if any are possible, to reduce the risk of harm that could result from it or to mitigate that harm. It must also contain any other prescribed information.

The notification must be conspicuous and must be given directly to the individual in the prescribed form and manner, except in prescribed circumstances, in which case it must be given indirectly in the prescribed form and manner.
The notification must be given as soon as feasible after the organization determines that the breach has occurred.

For the purposes of this section, significant harm includes bodily harm, humiliation, damage to reputation or relationships, loss of employment, business or professional opportunities, financial loss, identity theft, negative effects on the credit record and damage to or loss of property.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Real risk of significant harm â factors
Subsection.Text: The factors that are relevant to determining whether a breach of security safeguards creates a real risk of significant harm to the individual include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the sensitivity of the personal information involved in the breach;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the probability that the personal information has been, is being or will be misused; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: any other prescribed factor.

Subsection.Text: An organization that notifies an individual of a breach of security safeguards under subsection 58(3) must notify any other organization, a government institution or a part of a government institution of the breach if the notifying organization believes that the other organization or the government institution or part concerned may be able to reduce the risk of harm that could result from it or mitigate that harm, or if any of the prescribed conditions are satisfied.
The notification must be given as soon as feasible after the organization determines that the breach has occurred.
An organization must, in accordance with any prescribed requirements, keep and maintain a record of every breach of security safeguards involving personal information under its control.
An organization must, on request, provide the Commission with access to, or a copy of, the record.

Section.Text: If a service provider determines that any breach of security safeguards has occurred that involves personal information, it must as soon as feasible notify the organization that controls the personal information.

Subsection.Text: An organization must make readily available, in plain language, information that explains the organizationâs policies and practices put in place to fulfill its obligations under this Act.
In fulfilling its obligation under subsection (1), an organization must make the following information available:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a description of the type of personal information under the organizationâs control;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a general account of how the organization uses the personal information and of how it applies the exceptions to the requirement to obtain an individualâs consent under this Act, including a description of any activities referred to in subsection 18(3) in which it has a legitimate interest;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a general account of the organizationâs use of any automated decision system to make predictions, recommendations or decisions about individuals that could have a legal or similarly significant effect on them;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: whether or not the organization transfers or discloses personal information interprovincially or outside of Canada that may have reasonably foreseeable privacy implications;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the retention periods applicable to sensitive personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: how an individual may make a request for disposal under section 54 or access under section 63; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the business contact information of the individual to whom complaints or requests for information may be made.

Heading.TitleText: Access to and Amendment of Personal Information

Subsection.Text: On request by an individual, an organization must inform them of whether it has any personal information about them, how it uses the information and whether it has disclosed the information. It must also give the individual access to the information.
An organization is not required to act on a request in respect of de-identified personal information.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Names or types of third parties
Subsection.Text: If the organization has disclosed the information, the organization must also provide to the individual the names of the third parties or types of third parties to which the disclosure was made, including in cases where the disclosure was made without the consent of the individual.

If the organization has used an automated decision system to make a prediction, recommendation or decision about the individual that could have a legal or similarly significant effect on them, the organization must, on request by the individual, provide them with an explanation of the prediction, recommendation or decision.

The explanation must indicate the type of personal information that was used to make the prediction, recommendation or decision, the source of the information and the reasons or principal factors that led to the prediction, recommendation or decision.

The organization must provide an individual with an opportunity to make written representations to an employee of the organization who is able to review the prediction, recommendation or decision that was made about them.
A request made under section 63 must be made in writing.
An organization must assist any individual who informs the organization that they need assistance in preparing a request to the organization.

Section.Text: An organization may require the individual to provide it with sufficient information to allow the organization to fulfill its obligations under section 63.

Subsection.Text: The information referred to in section 63 must be provided to the individual in plain language.

For the purposes of section 63, an organization must give access to personal information in an alternative format to an individual with a sensory disability who requests that it be transmitted in that format if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a version of the information already exists in that format; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: its conversion into that format is reasonable and necessary in order for the individual to be able to exercise their rights under this Act.

Subsection.Text: An organization may choose to give an individual access to sensitive medical information through a medical practitioner.
An organization must respond to a request made under section 63 with due diligence and no later than 30 days after the day on which the request is received.
An organization may extend the time limit referred to in subsection (1)

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: for a maximum of 30 days if

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: meeting the time limit would unreasonably interfere with the activities of the organization, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the time required to undertake any consultations necessary to respond to the request would make the time limit impracticable to meet; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: for the period that is necessary in order to be able to convert the personal information into an alternative format.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: In either case, the organization must, no later than 30 days after the day on which the request was received, send a notice of extension to the individual, advising them of the new time limit, the reasons for extending the time limit and their right to make a complaint to the Commissioner in respect of the extension.

Subsection.Text: An organization that responds within the time limit and refuses a request must inform the individual in writing of the refusal, setting out the reasons and any recourse that they may have under section 73 or subsection 97(1).
If the organization fails to respond within the time limit, the organization is deemed to have refused the request.

Section.Text: An organization must not respond to an individualâs request made under section 63 at a cost unless

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the organization has informed the individual of the approximate cost;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the cost to the individual is minimal; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the individual has advised the organization that the request is not being withdrawn.

Section.Text: An organization that has personal information that is the subject of a request made under section 63 must retain the information for as long as is necessary to allow the individual to exhaust any recourse that they may have under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Despite section 63, an organization must not give an individual access to personal information under that section if doing so would likely reveal personal information about another individual. However, if the information about the other individual is severable from the information about the requester without imposing an undue burden on the organization, the organization must sever the information about the other individual before giving the requester access.
Subsection (1) does not apply if the other individual consents to the access or the requester needs the information because an individualâs life, health or security is threatened.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Information related to certain exceptions to consent
Subsection.Text: An organization must comply with subsection (4) if an individual requests that the organization

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: inform the individual about

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: any disclosure to a government institution or a part of a government institution under section 43, 44 or 45, subsection 46(1) or 47(1) or section 49, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the existence of any information that the organization has relating to a disclosure referred to in subparagraph (i), to a subpoena, warrant or order referred to in section 49 or to a request made by a government institution or a part of a government institution under section 43 or subsection 46(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: give the individual access to the information referred to in subparagraph (a)(ii).

Subsection.Text: An organization to which subsection (3) applies

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must, in writing and without delay, notify the government institution or the part of a government institution concerned of the request made by the individual; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must not respond to the request before the earlier of

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the day on which it is notified under subsection (5), and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: 30 days after the day on which the government institution or the part of a government institution is notified.

Subsection.Text: Within 30 days after the day on which it is notified under subsection (4), the government institution or part of a government institution must notify the organization as to whether the institution or part objects to the organization complying with the request. The institution or part of an institution may object only if the institution or part is of the opinion that compliance with the request could reasonably be expected to be injurious to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: national security, the defence of Canada or the conduct of international affairs;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the detection, prevention or deterrence of money laundering or the financing of terrorist activities; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the enforcement of federal or provincial law or law of a foreign jurisdiction, an investigation relating to the enforcement of any such law or the gathering of intelligence for the purpose of enforcing any such law.

Subsection.Text: Despite section 63, if an organization is notified under subsection (5) that the government institution or part of a government institution objects to the organization complying with the request, the organization

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: must refuse the request to the extent that it relates to paragraph (3)(a) or to information referred to in subparagraph (3)(a)(ii);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: must notify the Commission, in writing and without delay, of the refusal;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: must not give the individual access to any information that the organization has relating to a disclosure to a government institution or a part of a government institution under section 43, 44 or 45, subsection 46(1) or 47(1) or section 49 or to a request made by a government institution or part of a government institution under section 43 or subsection 46(1);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: must not provide to the individual the name of the government institution or part of a government institution to which the disclosure was made or its type; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: must not disclose to the individual the fact that the organization notified an institution or part of an institution under paragraph (4)(a), that the institution or part of an institution objects or that the Commission was notified under paragraph (b).

Subsection.Text: Despite section 63, an organization is not required to give access to personal information if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the information is protected by solicitor-client privilege or the professional secrecy of advocates and notaries or by litigation privilege;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to do so would reveal confidential commercial information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to do so could reasonably be expected to threaten the life or security of another individual;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the information was collected under subsection 38(1);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the information was generated in the course of a formal dispute resolution process; or

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the information was created for the purpose of making a disclosure under the Public Servants Disclosure Protection Act or in the course of an investigation into a disclosure under that Act.

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: However, in the circumstances described in paragraph (b) or (c), if giving access to the information would reveal confidential commercial information or could reasonably be expected to threaten the life or security of another individual, as the case may be, and that information is severable from any other information for which access is requested without imposing an undue burden on the organization, the organization must give the individual access after severing.

Subsection.Text: Subsection (7) does not apply if the individual needs the information because an individualâs life, health or security is threatened.

If an organization decides not to give access to personal information in the circumstances set out in paragraph (7)(d), the organization must, in writing, notify the Commission, and must provide any information that the Commission may specify.

If an individual has been given access to their personal information and demonstrates that the information is not accurate, up to date or complete, the organization must amend the information as required.
An organization is not required to amend de-identified personal information.
The organization must, if it is appropriate to do so, transmit the amended information to any third party that has access to the information.

If the organization and the individual do not agree on the amendments that are to be made to the information, the organization must record the disagreement and, if it is appropriate to do so, inform third parties that have access to the information of the fact that there is a disagreement.

Subject to the regulations, on the request of an individual, an organization must as soon as feasible disclose the personal information that it has collected from the individual to an organization designated by the individual, if both organizations are subject to a data mobility framework.
An organization is not required to disclose de-identified personal information.

An individual may make a complaint, or a request for information, to an organization with respect to its compliance with this Part. The organization must respond to any complaint or request that it receives.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Process for making complaint or request
Subsection.Text: An organization must make readily available information about the process for making a complaint or request.
An organization must investigate any complaint that it receives and make any necessary changes to its policies, practices and procedures as a result of the investigation.

Section.MarginalNote: Proportionality of technical and administrative measures
Section.Text: An organization that de-identifies personal information must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: consider, when applying technical and administrative measures to the information, the risk of an individual being identified; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: ensure that those measures are proportionate to the purpose for which the information is de-identified and the sensitivity of the personal information.

Section.Text: An organization must not use de-identified personal information, alone or in combination with other information, to identify an individual except

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to conduct testing of the effectiveness of security safeguards that it has put in place;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in circumstances where the personal information was de-identified solely for the purpose of protecting the information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in circumstances where the organization obtains the individualâs valid consent;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in circumstances where an exception in respect of the use of an individualâs personal information set out in section 18, 23, 24 or 26, subsection 27(2) or 29(2), section 30, subsection 38(2) or section 50 applies;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: in circumstances where the personal information is used solely for the purpose of anonymizing it under section 20;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: to comply with any requirements under this Act or under federal or provincial law;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: to conduct testing of the fairness and accuracy of models, processes and systems that were developed using de-identified personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: to conduct testing of the effectiveness of its de-identification processes;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: for a purpose or situation authorized by the Division under section 138; and

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: in any other prescribed circumstances.

Section.Text: For the purposes of this Act, the Commission may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: disclose to the Commissioner of Competition, under an agreement or arrangement entered into under section 80, information that relates to an entity that operates an approved certification program or an organization that is certified under an approved certification program;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: consult with federal government institutions respecting codes of practice or certification programs; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the form and manner that the Commission considers appropriate,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: develop and conduct information programs to foster public understanding of this Act and its purposes,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: develop guidance materials and tools for organizations in relation to their compliance with this Act â including any guidance materials and tools that are requested by the Minister â in consultation with stakeholders, including any relevant federal government institutions,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: undertake and publish research in relation to the protection of personal information, including any research that is requested by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: undertake and publish any research in relation to the operation or implementation of this Act that is requested by the Minister,

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: on request by an organization, provide guidance on â and, if the Commission considers it appropriate, recommend corrective measures in relation to â its privacy management program, and

Subparagraph.Label: (vi)
Subparagraph.Text: promote, by any other means that the Commission considers appropriate, the purpose of this Act.

Section.Text: In exercising any powers or performing any duties or functions under this Act, the Commission must take into account all relevant factors, including, if applicable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the size and revenue of organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the volume of the personal information under the control of organizations and the sensitivity of that information;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of children;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of respecting Canadaâs international trade obligations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of supporting economic growth, competition and innovation in the Canadian marketplace; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter of general public interest.

Section.MarginalNote: Information â powers, duties or functions

Subsection.Text: The Commission must make readily available information on the manner in which it, the Commissioner and the Division exercise their powers and perform their duties and functions under this Act.

The Commission must develop, in consultation with the Minister and stakeholders, guidance material respecting the exercise or performance of the powers, duties and functions of the Commission, the Commissioner and the Division in relation to enforcement and dispute resolution under this Act, including in respect of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the decision to initiate a complaint under subsection 97(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the handling of complaints, including the decision as to whether to initiate or discontinue an investigation and the conduct of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109 or an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the dispute resolution mechanisms referred to in section 101;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the compliance agreements referred to in section 102 and 103;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed orders referred to in subsections 107(3) and 108(3) and compliance orders referred to in subsection 110(1);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the administrative monetary penalty scheme; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the audits referred to in section 118.

Subsection.Text: The Commission must make the guidance material available in a form and manner that it considers appropriate.

Section.Text: The Commission may enter into an agreement or arrangement with the Minister that relates to the administration of this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Agreements or arrangements â CRTC, Commissioner of Competition, etc.

Subsection.Text: The Commission may enter into agreements or arrangements with the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission, the Commissioner of Competition or any other prescribed entity in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: coordinate the activities of their respective offices, including providing for mechanisms for handling complaints of mutual interest;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: undertake and publish research on issues of mutual interest; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: develop procedures for disclosing information referred to in subsection (2).

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: The Commission must, if the Commission considers it appropriate to do so, carry out those objectives in a manner that promotes regulatory consistency.

Subsection.Text: The Commission may, in accordance with any procedure developed under paragraph (1)(c), disclose information, other than information that it has received under subsection 10(1) or subparagraph 76(c)(v), to the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission, the Commissioner of Competition or a prescribed entity referred to in subsection (1) if the information is relevant to their powers, duties or functions.
The procedures referred to in paragraph (1)(c) must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: restrict the use of the information to the purpose for which it was originally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: stipulate that the information be treated in a confidential manner and that it not be further disclosed without the express consent of the Commission.

Subsection.Text: If the Commission considers it appropriate to do so, or on request by an interested person, the Commission may, in order to ensure that personal information is protected in as consistent a manner as possible, consult with any person who, under provincial legislation, has powers, duties and functions similar to its own, to those of the Commissioner or to those of the Division with respect to the protection of personal information.
The Commission may enter into agreements or arrangements with any person referred to in subsection (1) in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: coordinate the activities of their respective offices, including providing for mechanisms for handling complaints in which they are mutually interested;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: undertake and publish research, or develop and publish guidelines or other documents, related to the protection of personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: develop model contracts or other documents related to the protection of personal information that is collected, used or disclosed interprovincially or internationally; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: develop procedures for disclosing information referred to in subsection (3).

Subsection.Text: The Commission may, in accordance with any procedure developed under paragraph (2)(d), disclose information, other than information that it has received under subsection 10(1) or subparagraph 76(c)(v), to any person referred to in subsection (1) if the information

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: could be relevant to the â ongoing or potential â investigation of a complaint, proceeding in respect of an application referred to in section 109, proceeding in respect of an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4) or audit carried out under this Act or provincial legislation that has objectives that are similar to this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: could assist that person, the Commission, the Commissioner or the Division in the exercise of their powers or the performance of their duties or functions with respect to the protection of personal information.

Subsection.Text: The procedures referred to in paragraph (2)(d) must

Paragraph.Label: (a)
