Paragraph.Text: restrict the use of the information to the purpose for which it was originally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: stipulate that the information be treated in a confidential manner and that it not be further disclosed without the express consent of the Commission.

Section.MarginalNote: Disclosure of information to foreign state

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsection (3), the Commission may, in accordance with any procedure developed under paragraph (4)(b), disclose information referred to in subsection (2), other than information that it has received under subsection 10(1) or subparagraph 76(c)(v), to any person or body that, under the legislation of a foreign state, has

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: powers, duties and functions similar to those of the Commission, Commissioner or Division with respect to the protection of personal information; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: responsibilities that relate to conduct that is substantially similar to conduct that would be in contravention of this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Commission is authorized to disclose information that it believes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: could be relevant to an â ongoing or potential â investigation or proceeding in respect of a contravention of the laws of a foreign state that address conduct that is substantially similar to conduct that would be in contravention of this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is necessary to disclose in order to obtain from the person or body information that could be relevant to an â ongoing or potential â investigation, proceeding in respect of an application referred to in section 109, proceeding in respect of an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4) or audit carried out under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Commission may only disclose information to the person or body if the Commission has entered into a written arrangement with the person or body that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: limits the disclosure of information to what is necessary for the purpose set out in paragraph (2)(a) or (b);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: restricts the use of the information to the purpose for which it was originally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: stipulates that the information be treated in a confidential manner and that it not be further disclosed without the express consent of the Commission.

Subsection.Text: The Commission may enter into arrangements with one or more persons or bodies referred to in subsection (1) in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide for cooperation with respect to the enforcement of laws protecting personal information, including the disclosure of information referred to in subsection (2) and the provision of mechanisms for handling complaints of mutual interest;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: develop procedures for disclosing information referred to in subsection (2);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: develop recommendations, resolutions, rules, standards or other documents with respect to the protection of personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: undertake and publish research related to the protection of personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: share knowledge and expertise by different means, including through staff exchanges; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: identify issues of mutual interest and determine priorities pertaining to the protection of personal information.

Subsection.Text: The Commission must include in the report referred to in subsection 21(1) of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act information in respect of:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the exercise of the powers, and the performance of the duties and functions, of the Commission, the Commissioner and the Division under this Act, including in relation to paragraph 76(c), section 77, subsections 78(2) and 80(1) and sections 86 and 90;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the application of this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the extent to which the provinces have enacted legislation that is substantially similar to this Act and the application of any such legislation.

Subsection.Text: The Commission must consult with persons in the provinces who it believes are in a position to assist it in making a report on personal information that is collected, used or disclosed interprovincially or internationally.

The Commission may delegate, subject to any terms and conditions that it may specify, to any of its members, the Division or a division of the Commission established under section 8 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act any of the powers, duties and functions conferred on it under this Act or the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act, to the extent that those powers are exercised and those duties and functions are performed in connection with powers, duties and functions under this Act.

The Commission must not delegate its power referred to in section 9 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act to establish rules respecting its procedures and practices in connection with matters to which this Act applies, except to the Commissioner or to the Division.

The Commission must not delegate the powers, duties and functions referred to in subsection 110(1), 121(1) or 123(4), except to a division of the Commission established under section 8 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act or one or more members of the Commission other than the Commissioner.

Any person to whom powers, duties or functions are delegated must be given a certificate of the delegation and the delegate must produce the certificate, on request, to the person in charge of any premises to be entered under paragraph 121(2)(e).
The Governor in Council must designate one of the members of the Commission, other than the Chairperson, to be the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner.
The Commissioner may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: request that an entity that operates an approved certification program provide the Commissioner with information that relates to the program;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cooperate with an entity that operates an approved certification program for the purpose of the exercise of the Commissionerâs powers, and the performance of their duties and functions, under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: in the circumstances and according to the criteria set out in the regulations, recommend, in accordance with the regulations, to an entity that operates an approved certification program that an organizationâs certification be withdrawn if the Commissioner is of the opinion that the organization is not in compliance with the requirements of the program; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in accordance with the regulations, revoke an approval of a certification program in the circumstances and according to the criteria set out in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: If the Commissioner is absent or incapacitated or if their office is vacant, the Chairperson may authorize another member of the Commission to act as Commissioner. The member is not, however, authorized to act as Commissioner for a period of more than 90 days without the approval of the Governor in Council.

Section.Text: In exercising any powers or performing any duties or functions under this Act, the Commissioner must take into account all relevant factors, including, if applicable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the size and revenue of organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the volume of the personal information under the control of organizations and the sensitivity of that information;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of children;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of respecting Canadaâs international trade obligations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of supporting economic growth, competition and innovation in the Canadian marketplace; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter of general public interest.

Section.MarginalNote: Prohibition â use for initiating complaint or audit
Section.Text: The Commissioner must not use the information that the Commission receives under subsection 10(1) or subparagraph 76(c)(v) as grounds to initiate a complaint under subsection 97(2) or to carry out an audit under section 118, unless the Commissioner considers that the organization has wilfully disregarded the corrective measures that were recommended in relation to its privacy management program.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may delegate, subject to any terms and conditions that they may specify, any of the powers, duties and functions conferred on them, other than those referred to in subsection 105(1), section 106 and paragraph 122(1)(d),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to any employee of the Commission; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: with the consent of the Chairperson, to the Division, a division of the Commission established under section 8 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act or one or more other members of the Commission.

Subsection.Text: Any person to whom powers, duties or functions set out in paragraphs 122(1)(a) to (c) and (e) to (h) are delegated must be given a certificate of the delegation and the delegate must produce the certificate, on request, to the person in charge of any premises to be entered under paragraph (f) of that subsection.
The Privacy and Consumer Data Division is established and composed of the Commissioner and at least one other member of the Commission assigned by the Commission.

The members who compose the Division, other than the Commissioner, may continue to exercise any power and perform any duty or function that they are authorized to exercise or perform under any other Act of Parliament.

Section.Text: In exercising any powers or performing any duties or functions under this Act, the Division must take into account all relevant factors, including, if applicable,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the purpose of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the size and revenue of organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the volume and sensitivity of the personal information under the control of organizations;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the best interests of children;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of respecting Canadaâs international trade obligations;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the importance of supporting economic growth, competition and innovation in the Canadian marketplace; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any other matter of general public interest.

Subsection.Text: The Division may delegate to one or more members of the Commission, or to any of its employees, any of the powers, duties and functions conferred on the Division, subject to any terms and conditions that it may specify.
The Division must not delegate, without the consent of the Chairperson, any of its powers, duties or functions to a member of the Commission who has not been assigned to the Division.

Heading.TitleText: Codes of Practice and Certification Programs

Subsection.Text: In this section and sections 93 to 96, entity includes any organization, regardless of whether it is an organization to which this Act applies, or a government institution.

An entity may, in accordance with the regulations, apply to the Division for approval of a code of practice that provides for substantially the same or greater protection of personal information as some or all of the protection provided under this Act.
The Division may approve the code of practice if the Division determines that the code meets the criteria set out in the regulations.
An entity may, in accordance with the regulations, apply to the Division for approval of a certification program that includes

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a code of practice that provides for substantially the same or greater protection of personal information as some or all of the protection provided under this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guidelines for interpreting and implementing the code of practice;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a mechanism by which an entity that operates the program may certify that an organization is in compliance with the code of practice;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: a mechanism for the independent verification of an organizationâs compliance with the code of practice;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: disciplinary measures for non-compliance with the code of practice by an organization, including the revocation of an organizationâs certification; and

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: anything else that is provided in the regulations.

Subsection.Text: The Division may approve the certification program if the Division determines that the program meets the criteria set out in the regulations.

Section.Text: The Division must respond in writing to an application under subsection 92(2) or 93(1) in the time specified in the regulations.
The Division must make public a decision to approve a code of practice or certification program.
For greater certainty, compliance with the requirements of a code of practice or a certification program does not relieve an organization of its obligations under this Act.

Subsection.Text: An individual may file with the Commissioner a written complaint against an organization for contravening Part 1.
If the Commissioner is satisfied that there are reasonable grounds to investigate a matter under this Act, the Commissioner may initiate a complaint in respect of the matter.

A complaint that results from the refusal to grant a request made under section 63 must be filed within six months, or any longer period that the Commissioner allows, after the refusal or after the expiry of the time limit for responding to the request, as the case may be.
The Commissioner must give notice of a complaint to the organization against which the complaint was made, unless the Commissioner decides under section 99 not to carry out an investigation.

Heading.TitleText: Investigation of Complaints and Dispute Resolution

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner must carry out an investigation in respect of a complaint, unless the Commissioner is of the opinion that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the complainant should first exhaust grievance or review procedures otherwise reasonably available;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint could more appropriately be dealt with, initially or completely, by means of a procedure provided for under any federal law, other than this Act, or provincial law;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint was not filed within a reasonable period after the day on which the subject matter of the complaint arose;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint raises an issue in respect of which a certification program that was approved by the Division under subsection 93(2) applies and the organization is certified under that program;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: there is insufficient evidence to pursue the investigation;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint is trivial, frivolous or vexatious or is made in bad faith;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the organization has provided a fair and reasonable response to the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: the matter is already the subject of an ongoing investigation under this Act or proceeding in respect of an application referred to in section 109 or interim order referred to in subsection 123(4);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: the matter has already been the subject of a report or decision by the Commission or Commissioner, as the case may be;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: the matter is being or has already been addressed under a procedure referred to in paragraph (a) or (b);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: the matter is the subject of a compliance agreement referred to in subsection 102(1) or 103(2);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: an investigation or any further investigation is unnecessary having regard to all the circumstances of the complaint; or

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: a notice of contravention was served on the organization in relation to the matter or to any act referred to in the complaint.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner must notify the complainant and the organization of the Commissionerâs decision not to investigate the complaint or any act referred to in the complaint and give reasons for the decision. However, if the decision is made for any of the reasons set out in section 99, the Commissioner must not notify the organization.

The Commissioner may reconsider a decision not to investigate under subsection (1) if the Commissioner is satisfied that the complainant has established that there are compelling reasons to investigate.

Section.Text: Despite subsection 98(1), the Commissioner is not required to carry out an investigation in respect of an act referred to in a complaint if the Commissioner is of the opinion that the act, if proved, would constitute a contravention of any of sections 6 to 9 of An Act to promote the efficiency and adaptability of the Canadian economy by regulating certain activities that discourage reliance on electronic means of carrying out commercial activities, and to amend the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act, the Competition Act, the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and the Telecommunications Act or section 52.01 of the Competition Act or would constitute conduct that is reviewable under section 74.011 of that Act.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may discontinue the investigation of a complaint if the Commissioner has formed an opinion referred to in subsection 98(1) or section 99. The Commissioner must notify the complainant and the organization of the discontinuance and give reasons for the decision.
If the decision is made for any of the reasons set out in section 99, the Commissioner must not notify the organization.

Section.Text: The Division may attempt to resolve a complaint by means of a dispute resolution mechanism such as mediation and conciliation, unless the complaint is the subject of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109.

Subsection.Text: If, at any time after an investigation has been commenced, the Commissioner believes on reasonable grounds that an organization has committed, is about to commit or is likely to commit an act or omission that could constitute a contravention of Part 1, the Commissioner may enter into, with that organization, a compliance agreement aimed at ensuring compliance with this Act.
A compliance agreement may contain any terms that the Commissioner considers necessary to ensure compliance with this Act.
The Commissioner must not issue a notice of contravention in respect of any matter covered under the agreement.
For greater certainty, entering into a compliance agreement does not preclude the prosecution of an offence under this Act.

If, after a notice of contravention has been served under subsection 105(1), the Commissioner initiates negotiations to enter into a compliance agreement with the organization, the Commission must suspend proceedings in respect of the contravention in relation to the act or omission that is the subject of the negotiations and notify the complainant and the organization without delay.

If a compliance agreement is entered into with the organization, the Commission must terminate the proceedings commenced in respect of the contravention in relation to the act or omission that is the subject of agreement and notify the complainant and the organization without delay.
If the Commissioner and the organization fail to enter into a compliance agreement, the Commission must resume the proceedings and notify the complainant and the organization without delay.

Heading.TitleText: Conclusion of Investigation, Penalties and Orders

Section.Text: The Commissioner must notify the complainant and the organization of their decision and the reasons for it if, after concluding their investigation, they decide not to issue a notice of contravention.

Subsection.Text: After investigating a complaint, the Commissioner must cause to be served on the organization a notice of contravention if they have reasonable grounds to believe that the organization has contravened any provision of this Act, and the complaint is not the subject of dispute resolution under section 101 and has not been resolved or discontinued.
The Commissioner must notify the complainant of their decision.

Section.Text: If the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe that an organization is not complying with the terms of a compliance agreement entered into under subsection 102(1), they must cause a notice of contravention to be served on the organization.

Section.MarginalNote: Notice of contravention â investigation of a complaint

Subsection.Text: A notice of contravention referred to in section 105 must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the facts of the alleged contravention and the Commissionerâs reasons for believing there is a contravention;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act that have been contravened;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the penalty, if any, that the organization is liable to pay and the time and manner in which the penalty must be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed order, if any, that the Commissioner considers reasonably necessary to ensure compliance with this Act and the Commissionerâs reasons for it;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the organizationâs rights and obligations under this Act, including the right to apply for a review and to make representations to the Commission under section 109 within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, or within any further period set out in the notice, and the procedure for doing so;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that, if the organization does not pay the penalty, if any, in accordance with the notice or if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109, the organization will be deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109 in relation to a proposed order, the organization will be deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue and the proposed order will be made by the Commission.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner must notify the complainant that the notice of contravention was served and that they may apply for a review and make representations to the Commission under section 109 within the period set out in that notice.
The Commissioner may, if they consider it reasonably necessary to ensure compliance with this Act, propose to order the organization to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take any measure necessary in order to comply with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cease any act that contravenes this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: make public anything done or planned to be done to correct policies, practices or procedures that it put in place to comply with its obligations under this Act.

Subsection.Text: Subject to section 103, if the organization pays the penalty in accordance with the notice of contravention, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions of the Act at issue, the proposed order, if any, relating to the provisions in respect of which the penalty was paid is made, as proposed, by the Commission, proceedings in respect of those provisions are ended and the Commission must notify the complainant.

Subject to section 103, if the organization does not pay the penalty, if any, in accordance with the notice of contravention or if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue and liable to pay the penalty, if any, set out in the notice and the proposed order, if any, in respect of the contravention is made, as proposed, by the Commission. The Commission must notify the complainant of the deemed contravention.

Subject to section 103, if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109 in relation to a proposed order, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue in the order and the proposed order is made, as proposed, by the Commission. The Commission must notify the complainant of the deemed contravention.

Section.MarginalNote: Notice of contravention â compliance agreement

Subsection.Text: A notice of contravention referred to in section 106 must set out

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the facts of the alleged non-compliance with the compliance agreement and the Commissionerâs reasons for believing there is non-compliance;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the provisions of this Act that have been contravened;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the penalty, if any, that the organization is liable to pay and the time and manner in which the penalty must be paid;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed order, if any, that the Commissioner considers reasonably necessary to ensure compliance with this Act and the Commissionerâs reasons for it;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: a summary of the organizationâs rights and obligations under this Act, including the right to apply for a review and to make representations to the Commission under section 109 within 30 days after the day on which the notice is served, or within any further period set out in the notice, and the procedure for doing so;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that, if the organization does not pay the penalty, if any, in accordance with the notice or if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109, the organization will be deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue or to have not complied with the compliance agreement; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109 in relation to a proposed order, the organization will be deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue or to have not complied with the compliance agreement and the proposed order will be made by the Commission.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner must notify the complainant that the notice of contravention was served and that they may apply for a review and make representations to the Commission under section 109 within the period set out in that notice.
The Commissioner may, if they consider it reasonably necessary to ensure compliance with this Act, propose to order the organization to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take any measure necessary in order to comply with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cease any act that contravenes this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: comply with the compliance agreement that the organization entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: make public anything done or planned to be done to correct policies, practices or procedures that it put in place to comply with its obligations under this Act.

Subsection.Text: If the organization pays the penalty in accordance with the notice of contravention, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue, the proposed order, if any, relating to the provisions in respect of which the penalty was paid is made, as proposed, by the Commission, proceedings in respect of those provisions are ended and the Commission must notify the complainant.

If the organization does not pay the penalty, if any, in accordance with the notice of contravention or if neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue and liable to pay the penalty, if any, set out in the notice and the proposed order, if any, in respect of the contravention is made, as proposed, by the Commission. The Commission must notify the complainant of the deemed contravention.

If neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in section 109 in relation to a proposed order, the organization is deemed to have contravened the provisions at issue and the proposed order is made, as proposed, by the Commission. The Commission must notify the complainant of the deemed contravention.

Within the period set out in the notice of contravention, the complainant and the organization on which the notice was served may apply for a review of any determination in relation to the elements referred to in paragraphs 107(1)(a) to (d) or 108(1)(a) to (d), as the case may be, and may make representations to the Commission in accordance with the notice.
The Commission may conduct a hearing for the purpose of determining an application and may hold all or any part of the hearing in private.

The Commission is not bound by any legal or technical rules of evidence in conducting proceedings under subsection (1) and must deal with the matters set out in the notice as informally and expeditiously as the circumstances and considerations of fairness and natural justice permit.
The Commission must determine, on a balance of probabilities, whether the organization committed the contravention.

After considering any representations, the Commission may confirm, cancel or vary any determination that is the subject of proceedings under subsection 109(1), may decide whether or not to make the proposed order, if any, and vary its content before making it and may, to the extent it is reasonably necessary to do so in order to ensure compliance with this Act, order the organization to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: take any measure necessary in order to comply with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: cease any act that contravenes this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: comply with a compliance agreement that the organization entered into; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: make public anything done or planned to be done to correct policies, practices or procedures that it put in place to comply with its obligations under this Act.

Subsection.Text: If the Commission decides under subsection (1) that the organization did not commit the contravention, the proceedings in respect of it are ended.
The Commission must send a copy of the decision and the reasons for it to the complainant and the organization without delay.

If a member of the Commission who is assigned to hear, or is hearing, an application referred to in section 109, either alone or as a member of a panel, holds any pecuniary or other interest that could be inconsistent with the proper performance of their duties and functions in relation to the application, the member must disclose the interest to the Chairperson without delay. The member must also disclose, without delay, any reason why their participation could result in a reasonable apprehension of bias.
The Commissioner does not have jurisdiction to hear an application referred to in section 109.
For greater certainty, a member of the Commission does not lack jurisdiction to hear an application referred to in section 109 by reason only that they participate in the work of the Division.

Section.Text: The Commission must make rules respecting the conduct of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109, including the rules of evidence to be followed, and must make those rules publicly available.

Subsection.Text: A penalty may only be imposed on an organization in relation to a contravention of one or more of the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 9(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 11(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 12(1), (3) and (4);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: section 13;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 14(1);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 15(1) and (7);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: section 16;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 17(2);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: section 52;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 54(1) and (5);

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 56(1);

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 58(1) and (3);

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: section 61; and

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 62(1).

Subsection.Text: In determining the amount of a penalty, the following factors must be taken into account:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the nature and scope of the contravention;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any evidence that the organization exercised due diligence to avoid the contravention;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: whether the organization made reasonable efforts to mitigate or reverse the contraventionâs effects;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the organizationâs history of compliance with this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the organizationâs ability to pay the penalty and the likely effect that paying it would have on the organizationâs ability to carry on its business;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any financial benefit that the organization obtained from the contravention;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: any prescribed factor; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: any other relevant factor.

Subsection.Text: A penalty must not be imposed if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: at the time of the contravention of the provision in question, the organization was in compliance with the requirements of a certification program that was in relation to that provision and was approved by the Division under subsection 93(2);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for the act or omission that constitutes the contravention has been instituted against the organization; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the organization establishes that it exercised due diligence to prevent the contravention.

Subsection.Text: A penalty paid or recovered in relation to a contravention is payable to the Receiver General.

Section.Text: The maximum penalty for all of the contraventions found to have been committed by an organization following the completion of proceedings in relation to any one investigation is the greater of $10,000,000 and 3% of the organizationâs gross global revenue in its financial year before the one in which the penalty is imposed.
The purpose of a penalty is to promote compliance with this Act and not to punish.

Subsection.Text: The following amounts are debts due to His Majesty in right of Canada that are payable and that may be recovered by the Minister in the Federal Court:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the amount of any penalty set out in a notice of contravention, beginning on the day on which it is required to be paid in accordance with the notice, unless an application for a review is made under section 109 in the time and manner set out in the notice or a compliance agreement has been entered into under section 103;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if an application referred to in paragraph (a) is made,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any penalty set out in the Commissionâs decision, beginning on the day on which it is required to be paid in accordance with the decision, if the decision changes the amount of a penalty set out in the notice of contravention, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the decision does not change any such amount, the amount of any penalty set out in the notice of contravention, beginning on the day specified in the Commissionâs decision;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: if the Commissionâs decision is appealed under section 126,

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the amount of any penalty set out in the decision of the Federal Court, beginning on the day on which it is required to be paid in accordance with that decision, if that decision changes the amount of a penalty set out in the Commissionâs decision, or

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if the decision of the Federal Court does not change any such amount, the amount of any penalty set out in the Commissionâs decision, beginning on the day specified in the Courtâs decision; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: if the decision of the Federal Court is appealed, the amount of the penalty, if any, set out in the final decision after all rights of appeal have been exhausted.

Subsection.Text: No proceedings to recover a debt referred to in subsection (1) may be commenced more than five years after the day on which the debt becomes payable.
The Commission may issue a certificate for the unpaid amount of any debt referred to in section 116.
Registration of a certificate in the Federal Court has the same effect as a judgment of that Court for a debt of the amount set out in the certificate and all related registration costs.

Section.Text: The Commissioner may, on reasonable notice and at any reasonable time, carry out an audit of the personal information management practices of an organization if the Commissioner has reasonable grounds to believe that the organization has contravened, is contravening or is likely to contravene Part 1.

Subsection.Text: In the course of carrying out an audit, the Commissioner may require the audited organization to, subject to the conditions that the Commissioner may specify, cause to be prepared, and submit to the Commissioner, a report, by a third party that is approved by the Commissioner, on any matter that the Commissioner specifies relating to the organizationâs compliance with Part 1.
The Commissioner must specify the time within which the report is to be submitted.
If the Commissioner refuses to approve the third party designated by the organization, the Commissioner must inform the organization in writing of the refusal, setting out the reasons.
The organization must give all reasonable assistance to the third party and is responsible for the cost of the preparation of the report.

After carrying out an audit, the Commissioner must provide the audited organization with a report that contains the findings of the audit and any recommendations that the Commissioner considers appropriate.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Reports may be included in annual reports
Subsection.Text: The report may be included in a report made under section 83.

Heading.TitleText: Powers of the Commission and Commissioner â Investigations, Audits and Proceedings

Subsection.Text: In the course of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109, the Commission may, in exigent circumstances, make any interim order that the Commission considers appropriate.
The Commission may, in the course of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109 or an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4),

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: summon and enforce the appearance of persons before the Commission and compel them to give oral or written evidence on oath and to produce any records and things that the Commission considers necessary for the purposes of the proceedings, in the same manner and to the same extent as a superior court of record;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: administer oaths;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: receive and accept any evidence and other information, whether on oath, by affidavit or otherwise, that the Commission sees fit, whether or not it is or would be admissible in a court of law;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: order an organization that has information that is relevant to the proceedings to retain the information for as long as is necessary to allow the Commission to complete the proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: at any reasonable time, enter any premises, other than a dwelling-house, occupied by an organization on satisfying the organizationâs security requirements for the premises;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: converse in private with any person in any premises entered under paragraph (e) and otherwise make any inquiries in those premises that the Commission sees fit; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: examine or obtain copies of, or extracts from, records that are found in any premises entered under paragraph (e) and that contain any matter relevant to the proceedings.

Subsection.Text: The Commission or its delegate must, within a reasonable time, return to a person or an organization any record or thing produced by that person or organization under this section, but nothing precludes the Commission or delegate from again requiring that the record or thing be produced.
In carrying out an investigation of a complaint or an audit, the Commissioner may

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: summon and enforce the appearance of persons before the Commissioner and compel them to give oral or written evidence on oath and to produce any records and things that the Commissioner considers necessary to carry out the investigation or audit, in the same manner and to the same extent as a superior court of record;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: administer oaths;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: receive and accept any evidence and other information, whether on oath, by affidavit or otherwise, that the Commissioner sees fit, whether or not it is or would be admissible in a court of law;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: in exigent circumstances, issue a notice of proposed interim order;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: order an organization that has information that is relevant to the investigation or audit to retain the information for as long as is necessary to allow the Commissioner to carry out the investigation or audit;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: at any reasonable time, enter any premises, other than a dwelling-house, occupied by an organization on satisfying any security requirements of the organization relating to the premises;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: converse in private with any person in any premises entered under paragraph (f) and otherwise make any inquiries in those premises that the Commissioner sees fit; and

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: examine or obtain copies of or extracts from records found in any premises entered under paragraph (f) that contain any matter relevant to the investigation or audit.

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner or the Commissionerâs delegate must return to a person or an organization any record or thing that they produced under this section within a reasonable time, but nothing precludes the Commissioner or the delegate from again requiring that the record or thing be produced.
The notice of proposed interim order referred to in paragraph (1)(d) must include

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the proposed interim order;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the reasons for the proposed order;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the right of the organization and of the complainant to make representations to the Commission within 10 days after the day on which the notice is served, or within any further period set out in the notice, and the procedure for doing so; and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the fact that, if representations are not made in accordance with the notice, the proposed order will be imposed.

Subsection.Text: Within the period set out in the notice of proposed interim order, the complainant and the organization may make representations to the Commission in accordance with that notice.
The Commission may conduct a hearing for the purpose of considering the proposed interim order and the reasons for it and may hold all or any part of the hearing in private.

The Commission is not bound by any legal or technical rules of evidence in conducting proceedings under subsection (1) and must deal with the matter as informally and expeditiously as the circumstances and considerations of fairness and natural justice permit.
After considering the representations, the Commission must decide, on a balance of probabilities, whether or not to make the interim order and may vary its content before making it.
The Commission must send a copy of the decision and the reasons for it to the complainant and the organization without delay.
If neither the complainant nor the organization exercise the remedies referred to in paragraph 122(3)(c), the proposed interim order as proposed is made by the Commission.

If a member of the Commission who is assigned to hear, or is hearing, proceedings referred to in subsection 123(1), either alone or as a member of a panel, holds any pecuniary or other interest that could be inconsistent with the proper performance of their duties and functions in relation to the proceedings, the member must disclose the interest to the Chairperson without delay. The member must also disclose, without delay, any reason why their participation could result in a reasonable apprehension of bias.
The Commissioner does not have jurisdiction to hear proceedings referred to in subsection 123(1).
For greater certainty, a member of the Commission does not lack jurisdiction to hear proceedings referred to in subsection 123(1) by reason only that they participate in the work of the Division.

Section.Text: The Commission must make rules respecting the conduct of proceedings referred to in subsection 123(1), including the rules of evidence to be followed, and must make those rules publicly available.

Subsection.Text: A complainant or organization that is affected by any of the following orders or decisions may appeal it to the Federal Court:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a decision made under subsection 110(1);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: an order made under that subsection; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a decision made under that subsection to change the amount of the penalty.

Subsection.Text: The time limit for making an appeal is 30 days after the day on which the Commission makes the decision under subsection 110(1).
A complainant or organization that is affected by an interim order made under section 121 or subsection 123(4) may, with leave of the Federal Court, appeal the order to the Federal Court.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Time limit â leave to appeal
Subsection.Text: The time limit for making an application for leave to appeal is 30 days after the day on which the order is made.

Section.Text: The Federal Court may dispose of an appeal by dismissing it or by allowing it and, in allowing the appeal, the Federal Court may substitute its own finding, order or decision for the one under appeal or may refer the matter back to the Commission for reconsideration.

Subsection.Text: An order made by the Commission under subsection 107(4), (5) or (6) or 108(4), (5) or (6) may, for the purpose of its enforcement, be made an order of the Federal Court and is enforceable in the same manner as an order of that Court.

If an order made by the Commission under subsection 110(1) is not appealed to the Federal Court or an appeal of the order is dismissed by the Federal Court, the order may, for the purpose of its enforcement, be made an order of the Federal Court and is enforceable in the same manner as an order of that Court.

If an application for leave to appeal an interim order made under subsection 121(1) or 123(4) to the Federal Court is not made, a leave application in relation to the order is dismissed by the Federal Court or the application for leave in relation to the order is granted by the Federal Court but the appeal is dismissed, then the order may, for the purpose of its enforcement, be made an order of the Federal Court and is enforceable in the same manner as an order of that Court.

Section.Text: An order referred to in section 129 is made an order of the Federal Court by filing a certified copy of it with the Registrar of that Court.
The Federal Court may, in accordance with its rules, award costs. However, no costs may be awarded against the Commission unless the Federal Court is satisfied

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: that an award is necessary to maintain the publicâs confidence in the administration of justice; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: that the absence of an award would have a substantial adverse effect on the other partyâs ability to carry on business.

Subsection.Text: An individual who is affected by an act or omission by an organization that constitutes a contravention of this Act has a cause of action against the organization for damages for loss or injury that the individual has suffered as a result of the contravention if

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the Commissioner has made a finding under subsection 105(1) or section 106 that the organization has contravened this Act and

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the organization has paid the penalty and subsection 107(4) or 108(4) apply, as the case may be,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: the finding is not the subject of an application for review under section 109 and the time limit for applying for a review set out in a notice of contravention has expired,

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the Commission has confirmed or varied the finding under subsection 110(1),

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: the finding is not appealed and the time limit for making an appeal under subsection 126(2) has expired, or

Subparagraph.Label: (v)
Subparagraph.Text: the Federal Court has dismissed an appeal of the finding under section 128;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Federal Court has made a finding under section 128 that the organization has contravened this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a final decision dismissing any appeal of the finding, or confirming the finding that the organization has contravened this Act, has been made and all rights of appeal have been exhausted; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the Commissioner has entered into a compliance agreement with the organization under section 103 and the agreement does not provide for the payment of damages for that loss or injury.

Subsection.Text: If an organization has been convicted of an offence under section 145, an individual affected by the act or omission that gave rise to the offence has a cause of action against the organization for damages for loss or injury that the individual has suffered as a result of the act or omission.

For greater certainty, subsections (1) and (2) do not limit or affect an individualâs right to avail themselves of a civil remedy under another Act of Parliament, under an Act of a legislature of a province or at common law.
An action must not be brought later than two years after the day on which the individual becomes aware of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an action under subsection (1),

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: the Commissionerâs finding,

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is a review under section 109, the decision of the Commission, or

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: if there is an appeal, the final decision after all rights of appeal have been exhausted; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: in the case of an action under subsection (2), the conviction.

Subsection.Text: An action referred to in subsection (1) or (2) may be brought in the Federal Court or a superior court of a province.

If a certificate under section 38.13 or 38.41 of the Canada Evidence Act prohibiting the disclosure of personal information of a specific individual is issued before a complaint is filed by that individual under this Act in respect of a request for access to that information, the provisions of this Act respecting that individualâs right of access to their personal information do not apply to the information that is subject to the certificate.

Despite any other provision of this Act, if a certificate under section 38.13 or 38.41 of the Canada Evidence Act prohibiting the disclosure of personal information of a specific individual is issued after the filing of a complaint under this Act in relation to a request for access to that information

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: all proceedings under this Act in respect of that information, including an investigation, proceeding in respect of an application referred to in section 109, proceeding in respect of an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4), audit, appeal or judicial review, are discontinued;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the Commission, the Commissioner or the Division must not disclose the information and must take all necessary precautions to prevent its disclosure; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the Commission, the Commissioner and the Division must, within 10 days after the day on which the certificate is published in the Canada Gazette, return the information to the organization that provided the information.

Subsection.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner and the Division and every person acting on their behalf or under their direction, in exercising their powers and performing their duties and functions under this Act, must not disclose information subject to a certificate issued under section 38.13 or 38.41 of the Canada Evidence Act and must take every reasonable precaution to avoid the disclosure of that information.

The Commissioner must not delegate the investigation in respect of any complaint relating to information subject to a certificate issued under section 38.13 or 38.41 of the Canada Evidence Act except to one of a maximum of four employees of the Commission specifically designated by the Commissioner for the purpose of conducting that investigation.

The Commission must not delegate the consideration of an application for a review under section 109, or a proceeding in respect of an interim order referred to in subsection 123(4), that is in relation to information subject to a certificate issued under section 38.13 or 38.41 of the Canada Evidence Act except to one of a maximum of four employees of the Commission specifically designated by the Commission for the purpose of conducting that review or proceeding, as the case may be.

Section.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner, the Division and any person acting on their behalf or under their direction who receives or obtains information relating to any investigation under this Act must, with respect to access to and the use of that information, satisfy any security requirements applicable to, and take any oath of secrecy required to be taken by, persons who normally have access to and use of that information.

Subsection.Text: Subject to subsections (3) to (8), 80(2), 81(3) and 82(1), section 83, paragraph 85(2)(c), section 95, subsections 97(4), 98(2) and 100(1), sections 103 to 106 and subsections 107(2) and (4) to (6), 108(2) and (4) to (6), 110(3), 120(1) and 123(5), the Commission, the Commissioner, the Division and any person acting on their behalf or under their direction must not disclose any information that comes to their knowledge as a result of the exercise of any of their powers or the performance of any of their duties or functions under this Act, other than those referred to in subsection 58(1) or 60(2).

Subject to subsections (3) to (8), 80(2), 81(3) and 82(1), section 83, paragraph 85(2)(c), section 95, subsections 97(4), 98(2) and 100(1), sections 103 to 106 and subsections 107(2) and (4) to (6), 108(2) and (4) to (6), 110(3), 120(1) and 123(5), the Commission, the Commissioner, the Division and any person acting on their behalf or under their direction must not disclose any information contained in a report made under subsection 58(1) or in a record obtained under subsection 60(2).

The Commission, the Commissioner or the Division may, if they consider that it is in the public interest to do so, make public any information that comes to their knowledge in the exercise of any of their powers or the performance of any of their duties or functions under this Act.

The Commission, the Commissioner or the Division may disclose, or may authorize any person acting on their behalf or under their direction to disclose, information that in their opinion is necessary to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: carry out an investigation or audit under this Act, consider an application referred to in section 109 or hear a matter referred to in subsection 123(1); or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: establish the grounds for findings and recommendations contained in any decision or report made under this Act.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure in the course of proceedings
Subsection.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner or the Division may disclose, or may authorize any person acting on their behalf or under their direction to disclose, information in the course of

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for an offence under section 145;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for an offence under section 132 of the Criminal Code (perjury) in respect of a statement made under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a proceeding or an appeal before the Federal Court under this Act.

Subsection.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner and the Division may disclose to the Attorney General of Canada or of a province, as the case may be, information relating to the commission of an offence under any federal or provincial law on the part of an officer or employee of an organization if, in their opinion, there is evidence of an offence.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure of breach of security safeguards
Subsection.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner and the Division may disclose, or may authorize any person acting on their behalf or under their direction to disclose, to a government institution or a part of a government institution, any information contained in a report made under subsection 58(1) or in a record obtained under subsection 60(2) if they have reasonable grounds to believe that the information could be useful in the investigation of a contravention of any federal or provincial law that has been, is being or is about to be committed.

The Commission, the Commissioner and the Division disclose information, or may authorize any person acting on their behalf or under their direction to disclose information in accordance with subsection 58(3) or 60(1) of An Act to promote the efficiency and adaptability of the Canadian economy by regulating certain activities that discourage reliance on electronic means of carrying out commercial activities, and to amend the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act, the Competition Act, the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and the Telecommunications Act.

Section.Text: The Commission, the Commissioner or the Division or any person acting on their behalf or under their direction is not a competent witness in respect of any matter that comes to their knowledge as a result of the exercise of any of their powers or the performance of any of their duties or functions under this Act in any proceeding other than

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for an offence under section 145;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: a prosecution for an offence under section 132 of the Criminal Code (perjury) in respect of a statement made under this Act; or

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: a proceeding or an appeal before the Federal Court under this Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Protection of Commission, Commissioner and Division

Subsection.Text: No criminal or civil proceedings lie against the Commission, the Commissioner or the Division, or against any person acting on their behalf or under their direction, for anything done, reported, decided or said in good faith as a result of the exercise or purported exercise of any of their powers or the performance or purported performance of any of their duties or functions under this Act.
No action lies in defamation with respect to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: anything said, any information supplied or any record or thing produced in good faith in the course of an investigation or audit carried out, or proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109 or interim order referred to in subsection 123(4), conducted by or on behalf of the Commission, the Commissioner or the Division under this Act; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: any report or decision made in good faith by the Commission, the Commissioner or the Division under this Act and any fair and accurate account of the report or decision made in good faith for the purpose of news reporting.

Section.Text: For the purposes of paragraph 75(i), the Division may, on request by an organization, authorize a purpose or situation in which the organization may use de-identified personal information, alone or in combination with other information, to identify an individual if, in the Divisionâs opinion, it is clearly in the interests of the individual.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations for carrying out the purposes and provisions of this Act, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the anonymization of personal information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the scope of any of the activities set out in paragraphs 18(2)(a) to (c), including specifying activities that are not business activities for the purposes of subsection 18(1);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: specifying what is a government institution or part of a government institution for the purposes of any provision of this Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: specifying information for the purposes of section 50;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the disposal of personal information for the purposes of section 54;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting security safeguards for the purposes of section 56;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: specifying information to be kept and maintained under subsection 60(1);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: respecting an individualâs right of access to their personal information; and

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: prescribing anything that by this Act is to be prescribed.

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order,

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: provide that this Act is binding on any agent of His Majesty in right of Canada to which the Privacy Act does not apply;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: if satisfied that legislation of a province that is substantially similar to this Act applies to an organization, a class of organizations, an activity or a class of activities, exempt the organization, activity or class from the application of this Act in respect of the collection, use or disclosure of personal information that occurs within that province; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: amend the schedule by adding or deleting, in column 1, the name of an organization or by adding or deleting, in column 2, the description of personal information in relation to an organization in column 1.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Regulations â substantially similar provincial legislation
Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations establishing

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: criteria that are to be applied in making a determination under paragraph (2)(b) that provincial legislation is substantially similar to this Act, or in reconsidering that determination; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the process for making or reconsidering that determination.

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting the disclosure of personal information under section 72, including regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting data mobility frameworks and prescribing

Subparagraph.Label: (i)
Subparagraph.Text: safeguards that must be put in place by organizations to enable the secure disclosure of personal information under section 72 and the secure collection of that information, and

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: parameters for the technical means for ensuring interoperability in respect of the disclosure and collection of that information;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: specifying organizations that are subject to a data mobility framework; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: providing for exceptions to the requirement to disclose personal information, including exceptions related to the protection of proprietary or confidential commercial information.

Section.Text: Regulations made under subsection 139(1) or section 140 may distinguish among different classes of activities, government institutions or parts of government institutions, information, organizations or entities.

Section.MarginalNote: Regulations â codes of conduct and certification programs
Section.Text: The Minister may make regulations

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the criteria for and the manner and the circumstances in which a recommendation may be made under paragraph 85(2)(c);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the criteria for and the manner and the circumstances in which an approval may be revoked under paragraph 85(2)(d);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the making of an application under subsection 92(2);

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: setting out criteria for the purposes of subsection 92(3);

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reconsideration of a determination made under subsection 92(3);

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the making of an application under subsection 93(1);

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: providing for anything else that must be included in a certification program for the purposes of paragraph 93(1)(f);

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: setting out criteria for the purposes of subsection 93(2);

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: respecting the reconsideration of a determination made under subsection 93(2);

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: specifying, for the purposes of section 94, the time for responding to an application; and

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: respecting record-keeping and reporting obligations of an entity that operates an approved certification program, including obligations to provide reports to the Commission in respect of an approved certification program.

Subsection.Text: Any person who has reasonable grounds to believe that a person has contravened or intends to contravene Part 1 may notify the Commission of the particulars of the matter and may request that their identity be kept confidential with respect to the notification.
The Commission must keep confidential the identity of a person who has notified the Commission under subsection (1) and to whom an assurance of confidentiality has been provided by the Commission.
An employer must not dismiss, suspend, demote, discipline, harass or otherwise disadvantage an employee, or deny an employee a benefit of employment, by reason that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has disclosed to the Commission that the employer or any other person has contravened or intends to contravene Part 1;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has refused or stated an intention of refusing to do anything that is a contravention of Part 1;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the employee, acting in good faith and on the basis of reasonable belief, has done or stated an intention of doing anything that is required to be done in order that Part 1 not be contravened; or

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the employer believes that the employee will do anything referred to in paragraph (a), (b) or (c).

Subsection.Text: Nothing in this section impairs any right of an employee, either at law or under an employment contract or collective agreement.
In this section, employee includes an independent contractor and employer has a corresponding meaning.

Section.Text: Every organization that knowingly contravenes section 58, subsection 60(1), section 69 or 75 or subsection 144(1) or an order made under subsection 110(1), that obstructs the Commissioner or the Commissionerâs delegate in the investigation of a complaint or in carrying out an audit or that obstructs the Commission or the Commissionâs delegate in the course of proceedings in respect of an application referred to in section 109 or interim order referred to in subsection 123(4) is

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an indictable offence and liable to a fine not exceeding the greater of $25,000,000 and 5% of the organizationâs gross global revenue in its financial year before the one in which the organization is sentenced; or

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: guilty of an offence punishable on summary conviction and liable to a fine not exceeding the greater of $20,000,000 and 4% of the organizationâs gross global revenue in its financial year before the one in which the organization is sentenced.

Subsection.Text: Five years after the day on which this section comes into force, and every five years after that, a comprehensive review of the provisions and operation of this Act is to be commenced by a committee of the Senate, of the House of Commons or of both Houses of Parliament that may be designated or established by the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament, as the case may be, for that purpose.

Within one year, or any further time that is authorized by the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament, as the case may be, after the day on which the review is commenced, the committee must submit a report on that review to the Senate, the House of Commons or both Houses of Parliament, as the case may be, together with a statement of any changes recommended by the committee.

Section.Text: The provisions of this Act come into force on a day or days to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day or those days must not be before the day on which Part 3 of An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and to make amendments to other Acts comes into force.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act

Section.Text: The long title of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: An Act to provide for the use of electronic means to communicate or record information or transactions

Section.Text: Sections 1 to 30 of the Act are replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Electronic Documents Act.
Section 31 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (2):

Subsection.Text: The Governor in Council may, by order, designate a member of the Kingâs Privy Council for Canada as the Minister responsible for this Act.

Section.Text: Parts 3 to 5 of the Act are repealed.
Schedule 1 to the Act is repealed.
Schedule 4 to the Act is repealed.

Heading.Label: PART 3
Heading.TitleText: Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada

TitleText.XRefExternal: Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act

Section.Text: The long title of the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act is replaced by the following:

AmendedText.LongTitle: An Act to establish the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada

Section.Text: Section 1 of the Act is replaced by the following:
This Act may be cited as the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act.
The definition Commission in section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Commissionâmeans the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada established by section 4.â(Commission)

Section.Text: Section 4 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada is established.
Section 6 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

MarginalNote.XRefExternal: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act

Subsection.Text: The Commission exercises the powers and performs the duties and functions conferred on it under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.

Section.Text: Section 10 of the Act is replaced by the following:
The Commission consists of five full-time members to be appointed by the Governor in Council to hold office during good behaviour. The Governor in Council may remove a member at any time for cause.
Section 19 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Chairperson may, on behalf of the Commission, enter into contracts, memoranda of understanding or other agreements or arrangements â except agreements or arrangements referred to in section 120 of the Digital Safety Act or section 79 or subsection 80(1), 81(2) or 82(3) or (4) of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act â either in the name of His Majesty or in the name of the Commission.
Section 22 of the Act is replaced by the following:
A report referred to in subsection 21(1) must not contain personal information, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.
The definitions Commission and personal information in subsection 2(1) of the Digital Safety Act are replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Commissionâmeans the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada established by section 4 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act.â(Commission)
personal informationâhas the same meaning as in subsection 2(1) of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.â(renseignements personnels)

Section.Text: Paragraph 18(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: privacy rights and, to the extent it considers appropriate, the purpose of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act;

Section.Text: Section 122 of the Act is repealed.
The Act is amended by replacing âDigital Safety Commission of Canada Actâ with âDigital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Actâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of section 73 before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 75(1) before paragraph (a);

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the portion of subsection 117(1) before paragraph (a); and

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 119(2) and the portion of subsection 119(3) before paragraph (a).
