Heading.Label: PART 4
Heading.TitleText: Consequential and Related Amendments, Transitional Provision, Coordinating Amendments and Coming into Force

Subsection.Text: Schedule II to the Access to Information Act is amended by striking out the reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi sur la protection des renseignements personnels et les documents Ã©lectroniques

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and the corresponding reference to âsubsection 20(1.1)â.

Subsection.Text: Schedule II to the Act is amended by adding, in alphabetical order, a reference to

BilingualGroup.BilingualItemEn: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act
BilingualGroup.BilingualItemFr: Loi visant Ã protÃ©ger la vie privÃ©e et les donnÃ©es des consommateurs

ContinuedSectionSubsection.Text: and a corresponding reference to âsubsection 135(2)â.

Section.Text: Subsection 4.83(1) of the Aeronautics Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Despite Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to the extent that that Part relates to obligations relating to the disclosure of information, an operator of an aircraft departing from Canada that is due to land in a foreign state or fly over the United States and land outside Canada or of a Canadian aircraft departing from any place outside Canada that is due to land in a foreign state or fly over the United States may, in accordance with the regulations, provide to a competent authority in that foreign state any information that is in the operatorâs control relating to persons on board or expected to be on board the aircraft and that is required by the laws of the foreign state.

Section.Text: Item 14 of the schedule to the Canada Evidence Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: The Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, for the purposes of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act

Section.Text: Item 17 of the schedule to the Act is replaced by the following:

Provision.Text: A judge of the Federal Court, for the purposes of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act

TitleText.XRefExternal: Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act

Section.Text: The Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act is amended by adding the following after section 12:

Section.MarginalNote: Agreements or arrangements â Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada

Subsection.Text: The Commission may enter into an agreement or arrangement with the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: coordinate the activities of their respective offices, including providing for mechanisms for handling complaints of mutual interest;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: undertake and publish research on issues of mutual interest; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: develop procedures for disclosing information referred to in subsection (2).

Subsection.Text: The Commission may, in accordance with any procedure established under paragraph (1)(c), disclose information to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada if the information is relevant to its powers, duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.
The procedures referred to in paragraph (1)(c) shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: restrict the use of the information to the purpose for which it was originally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: stipulate that the information be treated in a confidential manner and not be further disclosed without the express consent of the Commission.

Section.Text: The Competition Act is amended by adding the following after section 29.2:

Section.MarginalNote: Agreements or arrangements â Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada

Subsection.Text: Despite subsection 29(1), the Commissioner may enter into an agreement or arrangement with the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada in order to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: coordinate the activities of their respective offices, including providing for mechanisms for handling complaints of mutual interest;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: undertake and publish research on issues of mutual interest; and

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: develop procedures for disclosing information referred to in subsection (2).

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner may, in accordance with any procedure established under paragraph (1)(c), disclose information to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada if the information is relevant to its powers, duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.
The procedures referred to in paragraph (1)(c) shall

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: restrict the use of the information to the purpose for which it was originally disclosed; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: stipulate that the information be treated in a confidential manner and not be further disclosed without the express consent of the Commissioner.

Section.Text: Subsection 21.1(5) of the Canada Business Corporations Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Within one year after the sixth anniversary of the day on which an individual ceases to be an individual with significant control over the corporation, the corporation shall â subject to any other Act of Parliament and to any Act of the legislature of a province that provides for a longer retention period â dispose of any of that individualâs personal information, as defined in subsection 2(1) of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, that is recorded in the register.

Section.Text: Subsection 17.91(4) of the Railway Safety Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act and provincial legislation
Subsection.Text: A company that collects, uses or communicates information under this section, section 17.31 or 17.94, subsection 28(1.1) or 36(2) or regulations made under section 17.95 may do so

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: despite Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to the extent that that Part relates to obligations relating to the collection, use, disclosure, retention and disposal of information; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: despite any provision of provincial legislation that is substantially similar to that Act and that limits the collection, use, communication or preservation of information.

Subsection.Text: Subsection 25.3(2) of the Broadcasting Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (4) to (5.1) and (7), if a person designates information as confidential and the designation is not withdrawn by that person, no person described in subsection (3) shall knowingly disclose the information, or knowingly allow it to be disclosed, to any other person in any manner that is intended or likely to make it available for the use of any person who may benefit from the information or use it to the detriment of any person to whose business or affairs the information relates.
Section 25.3 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure to Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada
Subsection.Text: The Commission may disclose designated information obtained by it in the exercise of its powers or the performance of its duties or functions under this Act to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada in accordance with section 12.1 of the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act.
Subsection 39(2) of the Telecommunications Act is replaced by the following:

Subject to subsections (4), (5) and (5.1) to (6), if a person designates information as confidential and the designation is not withdrawn by that person, no person described in subsection (3) shall knowingly disclose the information, or knowingly allow it to be disclosed, to any other person in any manner that is calculated or likely to make it available for the use of any person who may benefit from the information or use the information to the detriment of any person to whose business or affairs the information relates.
Section 39 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (5.1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure to Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada
Subsection.Text: The Commission may disclose designated information obtained by it in the exercise of its powers or the performance of its duties or functions under this Act to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada in accordance with section 12.1 of the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act.

Section.Text: Paragraph 15(a) of the Public Servants Disclosure Protection Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to the extent that that Part relates to obligations relating to the disclosure of information; and

Section.Text: Subsection 16(1.1) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Subsection (1) does not apply in respect of information the disclosure of which is subject to any restriction created by or under any Act of Parliament, including the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.

Section.Text: Section 50 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Despite Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to the extent that that Part relates to obligations relating to the disclosure of information, and despite any other Act of Parliament that restricts the disclosure of information, a report by a chief executive in response to recommendations made by the Commissioner to the chief executive under this Act may include personal information within the meaning of subsection 2(1) of that Act, or section 3 of the Privacy Act, depending on which of those Acts applies to the portion of the public sector for which the chief executive is responsible.

Heading.TitleText: Chapter 23 of the Statutes of Canada, 2010

Subsection.Text: The definition Privacy Commissioner in subsection 1(1) of An Act to promote the efficiency and adaptability of the Canadian economy by regulating certain activities that discourage reliance on electronic means of carrying out commercial activities, and to amend the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission Act, the Competition Act, the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and the Telecommunications Act is replaced by the following:

Definition.Text: Privacy and Consumer Data Commissionerâmeans the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner, as designated under section 85 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.â(Commissaire Ã la protection de la vie privÃ©e et des donnÃ©es des consommateurs)

Subsection.Text: Subsection 1(1) of the Act is amended by adding the following in alphabetical order:

Definition.Text: Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canadaâmeans the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada referred to in section 4 of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada Act.â(Commission canadienne de la sÃ©curitÃ© numÃ©rique et de la protection des donnÃ©e)
Divisionâmeans the Privacy and Consumer Data Division established under section 89 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.â(Section)

Section.Text: Section 2 of the Act is replaced by the following:

In the event of a conflict between a provision of this Act and a provision of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, the provision of this Act operates despite the provision of that Act, to the extent of the conflict.
Paragraph 20(3)(c) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the personâs history with respect to

Subparagraph.Text: any previous violation of this Act,
any previous conduct that is reviewable under section 74.011 of the Competition Act,

any previous contravention of section 5 of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act, as it read immediately before the day on which section 3 of An Act to enact the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, to amend the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act and to make amendments to other Acts comes into force, that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 7.1(2) or (3) of the Personal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Act, as it read immediately before that day, and

Subparagraph.Label: (iv)
Subparagraph.Text: any previous contravention of Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act;

Section.Text: Sections 52 to 54 of the Act are replaced by the following:

An officer, director or agent or mandatary of a corporation that commits a contravention of any of sections 6 to 9 of this Act or a contravention of Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act, or that engages in conduct that is reviewable under section 74.011 of the Competition Act, is liable for the contravention or reviewable conduct, as the case may be, if they directed, authorized, assented to, acquiesced in or participated in the commission of that contravention, or engaged in that conduct, whether or not the corporation is proceeded against.

A person is liable for a contravention of any of sections 6 to 9 of this Act or a contravention of Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act, or for conduct that is reviewable under section 74.011 of the Competition Act, that is committed or engaged in, as the case may be, by their employee acting within the scope of their employment or their agent or mandatary acting within the scope of their authority, whether or not the employee or agent or mandatary is identified or proceeded against.

Subsection.Text: A person must not be found to have committed a contravention of any of sections 6 to 9 of this Act or a contravention of Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act, or to have engaged in conduct that is reviewable under section 74.011 of the Competition Act, if they establish that they exercised due diligence to prevent the contravention or conduct, as the case may be.

Every rule and principle of the common law that makes any circumstance a justification or excuse in relation to a charge for an offence applies in respect of a contravention or conduct referred to in subsection (1), to the extent that it is not inconsistent with this Act, the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act or the Competition Act, as the case may be.
The portion of section 56 of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.Text: Any organization to which the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act applies may on its own initiative disclose to the Commission, the Commissioner of Competition or the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada any information in its possession that it believes relates to

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 56(a)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act, which contravention relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act, or

Section.Text: Section 57 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission, the Commissioner of Competition and the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission must consult with each other to the extent that they consider appropriate to ensure the effective regulation, under this Act, the Competition Act, the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act and the Telecommunications Act, of commercial conduct that discourages the use of electronic means to carry out commercial activities, and to coordinate their activities under those Acts as they relate to the regulation of that type of conduct.

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(1)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, if the information relates to the exercise of the powers of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner or the Division or the performance of its duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act in respect of a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act; and

Subsection.Text: Paragraph 58(2)(a) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, if the Commissioner of Competition believes that the information relates to the exercise of the powers of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner or the Division or the performance of its duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act in respect of a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act; and

Subsection.Text: The portion of subsection 58(3) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Disclosure by Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada
Subsection.Text: The Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada may disclose information obtained by it in the exercise of its powers or the performance of its duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act if the information relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act or to an act alleged in a complaint in respect of which the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner decides, under section 99 of that Act, to not conduct an investigation or to discontinue an investigation,

Section.Text: Subsection 59(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.MarginalNote: Use of information by Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada
Subsection.Text: The Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada may use the information that is disclosed to it under paragraph 58(1)(a) or (2)(a) only for the purpose of exercising the powers of the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner or the Division or performing its duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act in respect of a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act.
The portion of subsection 60(1) of the Act before paragraph (a) is replaced by the following:

Section.MarginalNote: Information shared with the government of a foreign state, etc.

Subsection.Text: Information may be disclosed under an agreement or arrangement in writing between the Government of Canada, the Commission, the Commissioner of Competition or the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada and the government of a foreign state, an international organization of states or an international organization established by the governments of states, or any institution of any such government or organization, if the person responsible for disclosing the information believes that
Subparagraph 60(1)(a)(ii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (ii)
Subparagraph.Text: conduct that contravenes Part 1 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act and that relates to a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act,

Subsection.Text: Subparagraph 60(1)(b)(iii) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subparagraph.Label: (iii)
Subparagraph.Text: the exercise by the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, the Privacy and Consumer Data Commissioner or the Division of its powers or the performance of its duties or functions under the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act in respect of a collection or use described in subsection 51(2) or (3) of that Act, or

Subsection.Text: Subsection 60(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

An arrangement referred to in subsection (1) entered into by the Commission or the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada may be in respect only of contraventions of the laws of a foreign state that have consequences that would not be considered penal under Canadian law.
Subsection 60(5) of the Act is replaced by the following:

For the purposes of this section, an arrangement may be created by the acceptance, by the Government of Canada, the Commission, the Commissioner of Competition or the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada, of a written request for assistance from the government of a foreign state, an international organization of states or an international organization established by the governments of states, or any institution of any such government or organization, if the request is accompanied by a declaration that the party making the request will provide assistance to the party to whom the request is made on a reciprocal basis.

Section.Text: Section 61 of the Act is replaced by the following:

The Commission, the Commissioner of Competition and the Digital Safety and Data Protection Commission of Canada must provide the Minister of Industry with any reports that the Minister requests for the purpose of coordinating the implementation of sections 6 to 9 of this Act, sections 52.01 and 74.011 of the Competition Act and section 51 of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.

Section.MarginalNote: Replacement of âPersonal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Actâ
Section.Text: Every reference to the âPersonal Information Protection and Electronic Documents Actâ is replaced by a reference to the âElectronic Documents Actâ in the following provisions:

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the definition secure electronic signature in section 31.8 of the Canada Evidence Act;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 95(2) of the Canadian Forces Superannuation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: subsections 252.6(2) and (3) of the Canada Business Corporations Act;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: subsection 74(2) of the Public Service Superannuation Act;

Paragraph.Label: (e)

Section.Text: The Governor in Council may make regulations respecting any transitional matter arising from the coming into force of the provisions of the Protecting Privacy and Consumer Data Act.

Subsection.Text: In this section, âother Actâ means the Budget 2025 Implementation Act, No. 1.
If section 3 of this Act comes into force before Division 23 of Part 5 of the other Act, then that Division 23 is repealed.
If section 3 of this Act comes into force on the same day as Division 23 of Part 5 of the other Act, then that Division 23 is deemed never to have come into force and is repealed.

The provisions of this Act, other than sections 1 and 51 and Part 3, come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must not be before the day on which that Part 3 comes into force.

If Bill C-34, introduced in the 1st session of the 45th Parliament and entitled the Safe Social Media Act, receives royal assent, then Part 3 of this Act comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, but that day must not be before the day on which section 4 of the Digital Safety Commission of Canada Act, as enacted by section 4 of the Safe Social Media Act, comes into force.

row.entry: Personal information that the organization collects, uses or discloses in the course of its interprovincial or international activities
